## [00:00 - 03:00]

Alright, well now I'll be covering the product review, cross sales and up sales. One thing I wanted to point out is cross sales and up sales typically kind of are confusing for people. So instead, I've blubbed them into one flow and it doesn't really matter whether you're cross selling or upselling. What matters is that you're selling. People get cut up on the terms, more focused on actually implementing than anything. So for example, if your company sells watches, if you just sell a watch to someone, instead of trying to go for a cross sell or up sell, it probably just makes sense to sell people a different color wristband or a strap versus trying to sell them just a more expensive watch just because you want to do an up sell. So again, make sure you're focused on selling versus classifying whether it's an up sell or cross sell and at a later point in stage, if you want to send a campaign or something specific to a cross sell or up sell, I think that's a better point to try to go for it versus trying to get hung up on whether it's a cross sell or up sell right now. So first starting out with the reviews, I kind of want to show you a couple examples of what those emails look like and then we'll jump into cross selling, up selling and all that. So on my screen, as you can see, this is a product that I personally purchased and it's asking me what do I think. So it's addressing me by my name, thanking me for the purchase, showing the item that I purchased and asking for a review. So this is a really simple way to get started straight to the point. So definitely go mimic something like this right now. Here's another example by Casper asking how did you sleep? So these are people that purchased a Casper mattress and again, just asking for a review, trying to collect user generated feedback and content that then as a brand you can leverage in all your emails. Okay, and then one last example is just, thank you for your purchase. Again, here's the product, right review and asking people to share on social. So the very bottom you see where it says the picture is worth a thousand words, make sure to use our hashtag and that type of thing usually helps. So here's an example from one of our clients, again, calling Blanket and this message is from the founder asking people if they like the email to please leave a review. In this case, this actually links to a third party site called product reviews. The goal there is a lot of people from Australia, which is where this brand is based, use sites like this when they're considering brands to make a purchase from. So he's trying to drive reviews to not just his own site, but to external sites to be able to drive social proof and inbound sales. Here's another example by the same brand. So for him to leverage in assets for Facebook, Instagram, et cetera, having video testimonials from customers is really key to driving traffic and converting people. So here he's actually offering a hundred dollar refund on a product if you leave him a video review. So all you have to do is take a video of yourself and leave it in his folder and then he will give you a hundred dollar refund on your purchase. So a really powerful way to incentivize people to make really high quality reviews.

## [03:00 - 06:03]

Awesome. So now that you know kind of how to do the reviews and what those look like, now let's talk about Cross Sell and Upsell. The reason I lump these into one flow is it's best practice to typically ask for a review and then try to sell more. Instead of trying to sell more and then ask for a review, I think that order is better. So what is an upsell? At a basic level, an upsell is basically selling a product that is more expensive than the one originally sold. And a Cross Sell is selling products that are complementary or similar, not necessarily more expensive. So again, those are kind of just definitions as mentioned before. Just focus on selling. Don't spend too much time thinking about whether this is a Cross Sell or an upsell. Just selling in general moves the needle. With almost all of our clients, we never really differentiate. We're just focused on selling and we see that work. So here's an example from Common Blanket on how they're leveraging content and also a Cross Sell or an upsell opportunity to sell a different product. So in this email, you'll see that they're basically sending over three helpful sleep tips with the blanket. So leave your phone in the room. It's kind of a great way to unwind, keep an eye out on what you eat and when you eat it. And the last one, which is give aroma therapy a chance, this ties into one of their other products. So they're kind of natively integrating a Cross Sell or an upsell recommendation into content they're producing. This content makes for really great emails. So the whole goal in post purchase and email in general is to really provide insight and value to your customers. So again, if you have content that you could leverage for either educational or blog type post or a place like this where you can sell and do both, it's a really great way to do it. As with a product like this, that's kind of $200 to $300 to $400. These types of products take a lot more education and hand-holding. So make sure kind of in your post purchase, if you have other products you want to introduce, that you spend adequate time introducing it and kind of getting all the benefits and the pros to it because it does take time to sell more expensive products. So this example again is from Common Blanket and they have another product line called the Udi. In this email, they're basically telling their customers that they know who purchased blankets and letting them know about this Udi product, which is very comparable. It's just a sweater instead of a blanket. So because people are obviously interested in the blanket already, they're most likely going to also be interested in this sweater. And this email does a pretty good job of selling more Udis. So I'm about to show you a campaign email actually. We use this to be able to figure out whether this would make sense in a flow or not. And basically what we did is this company, which is a bad company, they introduced a new SKU called the ShopperTilt. So we basically took everyone that had bought a product from them and tried selling them that product first. And this is what the email looked like. So I had a nice little gift. I had a header kind of talked about the importance of this new product and kind of how to use it and explained it a little bit better. So you can see this email looks really nice and does a great job selling. And this email actually had a 50% open rate, almost a 7% click through and drove about

## [06:03 - 07:41]

$1,200 in revenue. So we also took that same campaign and tweaked the content a little bit and sent it to anyone who had not bought those similar SKUs. And you can see here a lot of the content is similar, but the way that we're speaking to the customer is a little bit different. And again, this email performed pretty well as well. So this email had about a 28% open rate, about a 2% click through, and it had about $1,200 in revenue. So again, speaking and sending content to your customers typically performs better as you can see here. So one point worth mentioning is for the Cross-Sell and Upsell, by having them kind of be one of the same, it makes it less complicated for you to actually have to figure out whether someone received a Cross-Sell or Upsell previously so that way you can do the opposite. So instead of doing that, combined into one, that way you don't have to worry about sending your customers two different sales emails for a Cross-Sell or Upsell. This reduces the number of emails and also gets the job done. So to quickly recap, again, the main focus here is on selling. Focus on products that are complimentary or trying to distinguish between a Cross-Sell or Upsell. It's just most important to execute and to start selling. And much like you see on Amazon when you're about to buy something, they're most focused on selling you products that are related or complimentary, more or less they don't really care too much about whether it's more expensive or not. So I would focus on those things where this is a great opportunity to again collect feedback, for reviews, make sure customers are happy, leverage this in other emails, and again, a great opportunity to sell more products. And don't forget to download the Cross-Sell or Upsell worksheet. This will help you map out what products of yours should be sold together and which one should be sold separately.
